theory.technical_interpretation: Definition of χ(ω) is a variational / legendre derivative, written χ(ω) = (∂ M)/(∂ H) |_ω, ω^2 χ(ω) = ω_0^2 + iγω - k^2 v_A^2 where 𝑣 𝐴 = 𝐵 / 𝜇 0 𝜌. It arises from a variational or Legendre procedure, defining conjugate quantities or stationarity conditions. It is built from ∂, ω, χ, γ, π, θ, defining χ(ω). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

latex_blocks: \chi(\omega) = \frac{\partial M}{\partial H}\Big|_\omega,\quad \omega^2 \chi(\omega) = \omega_0^2 + i\gamma\omega - k^2 v_A^2 where 𝑣 𝐴 = 𝐵 / 𝜇 0 𝜌 v A ​ =B/ μ 0 ​ ρ ​ (Alfvén speed) dynamic_tensor . → Suggests resonant field breathing and possible magnetoacoustic coupling. 4. Energy and Density Formulation Magnetic energy density: u_m = \frac{1}{2\mu_0} B^2,\quad U_{\text{total}} = \int_0^R\!\!\int_0^\pi\!\!\int_0^{2\pi} u_m r^2 \sin\theta\, dr\, d\theta\, d\phi
